summary: Revised proposal addresses all prior review feedback. Now has 31 criteria (26 feature + 5 regression) with comprehensive coverage of F17: Frozen Tundra. All four required revisions resolved: C-26 added for fire-lure ice melt puzzle, C-06 updated with platform-freezing tests, C-07 updated with programmatic golem kill, C-09 updated with left-edge death test. All three suggestions also addressed: C-11/C-23 width overlap fixed, C-18 explicit update ticks added, C-10 fork detection improved. Contract is ready for implementation.

previousReviewResolution.requiredRevision1: RESOLVED — C-26 added for fire-lure ice melt puzzle. Tests fire source existence and meltable ice block melt capability.
previousReviewResolution.requiredRevision2: RESOLVED — C-06 now includes test step checking freezesPlatforms flag, freezeOnHit, snowballFreeze, and freeze projectile type.
previousReviewResolution.requiredRevision3: RESOLVED — C-07 now uses page.evaluate to set golem.health = 0, call golem.die(), run update ticks, and check for shatter state/fragments.
previousReviewResolution.requiredRevision4: RESOLVED — C-09 now includes step that positions Player.x = Camera.x - 100 and verifies hpAfter < hpBefore or isDead === true.
previousReviewResolution.suggestion1: RESOLVED — C-11 minimum width raised from 110 to 115 to avoid overlap with Desert stage 2-1. C-23 now compares level name/ID and tile data instead of width.
previousReviewResolution.suggestion2: RESOLVED — C-18 now runs explicit update ticks: for (let i = 0; i < 60; i++) Enemies.updateBoss(1/60) before checking boss.phase.
previousReviewResolution.suggestion3: RESOLVED — C-10 now checks Level.forkSections and Level.pathForks arrays first before falling back to tile analysis heuristic.

criteriaReview.notes: Tundra tile palette check is specific (iceBlue '#A8D4E6', snowWhite '#E8F0F8', deepIce '#6B9CB8') and testable via page.evaluate + screenshot. Dual verification approach is solid.
Parallax layer count check via multiple property paths (Camera.parallaxLayers, Camera.layers, debug API). Aurora borealis verified via screenshot. Threshold clear.
Snow particle test uses dual-screenshot approach to verify animation. testMethod 'screenshot' is appropriate for visual particle effects.
Checks ice tile existence via multiple paths (TILE_ICE, TILES.ICE, Level.iceZones) and ice physics properties. Property existence check is pragmatic for friction mechanics.
Checks ice block existence in stage 3-2 via Level.iceBlocks and Level.pushableBlocks. Verifying existence is reasonable scope for contract.

Now includes three-part verification: Frost Imp existence (HP >= 1), projectile/snowball capability, AND platform-freezing behavior (freezesPlatforms, freezeOnHit, snowballFreeze, freeze projectile type). All prior review gaps addressed.
Now uses programmatic kill via page.evaluate (set health=0, call die(), run 10 update ticks). Checks for fragments array, shattersOnDeath flag, or shatter/dying state. Prior untestable step resolved.
Checks for flight pattern properties (flightTimer, swoopTimer, behavior type). Figure-8 pattern verified via behavior property. Reasonable for automated testing.

Now has complete three-part test: auto-scroll enabled (property check), camera advances automatically (60 frame wait), AND player takes damage/dies when positioned behind left edge (Player.x = Camera.x - 100, verify HP decrease or death). All prior review gaps addressed.
Now checks Level.forkSections/Level.pathForks arrays first before falling back to tile analysis heuristic. Primary check is reliable; fallback is a reasonable supplement.
Width >= 115 (raised from 110 to avoid overlap with Desert 2-1). Checks spawn, exit, boss data, and enemy count.
Width >= 130 matches spec. Complete level verification with spawn, exit, boss, enemies.
Width >= 160 matches spec. Complete level verification.
Frost Bear: maxHealth === 7, type check, HUD name verification. Frost beam mentioned in threshold — evaluator should verify attack pattern property exists.
Crystal Witch: accepts either maxHealth=25 or shieldHP=20+health=5 split. Good implementation flexibility. Shield property existence verified.
Yeti Monarch: maxHealth === 9. Boulder throw mentioned in threshold — evaluator should verify attack pattern property.
Strong behavioral test: sets vulnerable=false → attacks → no damage; sets vulnerable=true → attacks → damage reduced. Actual functional verification, not just property check.
Now runs explicit update ticks (60 iterations of Enemies.updateBoss(1/60) or Enemies.update(1/60)) after setting boss health to 40%. Properly triggers phase transition logic.
Boss defeat → STAGE_COMPLETE. Programmatic kill (set health=1, vulnerable=true, attack) then verify state transition.
Specific coin counts (20, 24, 30) matching WorldMap.STAGES definitions. Health pickup >= 1 per stage.
Category labeled 'Collectibles' but tests enemy placement (>= 3 per stage). Minor mislabel does not affect testability.
Two-state progression test: locked without World 2 completion, unlocked after stages 1-1 through 2-3 completed.
Now compares level name/ID and first-row tile data instead of width only. Prevents false passes from identically-sized but different levels. Width overlap issue fully resolved.
Boss fight trigger verification: boss exists with correct type and HP, HUD boss bar active.
Full integration flow: stage completion → world map update → save persistence with completedStages, bestTimes, coinRecords.

New criterion addressing prior review gap. Tests fire source existence, meltable ice block existence, and melt capability (function or state property). Covers the fire-lure ice melt puzzle from F17 spec.
World 1 regression: Forest stages load with correct widths (80, 120, 150), enemies spawn, boss HP values match (5, 6, 7).
World 2 regression: Desert stages load with correct widths (100, 130, 140), enemies spawn, boss HP values match (6, 8, 12).
Player movement regression in tundra context: walk, jump, attack, coyote time, wall slide.
State machine regression: TITLE → WORLD_MAP → STAGE → PAUSED flow. HUD rendering check.
Save system cross-world persistence. Critical regression test ensuring World 3 additions don't corrupt existing save data.

specFeaturesCovered: Frost Imp (snowball projectiles, freezes platforms) — C-06
Ice Golem (slides on ice, shatters on death, 2+ HP) — C-07
Stage 3-1 Frozen Lake level design — C-11
Stage 3-2 Crystal Caverns level design — C-12
Stage 3-3 Avalanche Peak level design — C-13
Frost Bear boss (7 HP, frost beam) — C-14
Crystal Witch boss (25 HP, crystal shield) — C-15
Yeti Monarch boss (9 HP, tiered arena, boulder throw) — C-16
World 3 locked until World 2 complete — C-22
Distinct level layouts per tundra stage — C-23
Stage completion + world map + save integration — C-25
specFeaturesNotExplicitlyCovered: Ice block bridge puzzle (F17 spec mentions it — partially covered by C-05 ice block sliding mechanic, which is the underlying system)
Frost Bear frost beam attack pattern (mentioned in C-14 threshold but no programmatic verification of beam mechanic)
Yeti Monarch boulder throw attack pattern (mentioned in C-16 threshold but no programmatic verification)
Yeti Monarch tiered arena layout (mentioned in C-16 description but no explicit platform height test)
coverageAnalysis.regressionCoverage: Complete — C-27 (Forest), C-28 (Desert), C-29 (Player movement), C-30 (State machine/HUD), C-31 (Save system) cover all critical regression vectors. Regression sprints [1,2,3,4,5,6] correctly listed.

minorNotes: C-21 category is labeled 'Collectibles' but tests enemy placement. Minor mislabel, not blocking.
C-07 description mentions Ice Golem 'slides on ice surfaces' but test steps verify HP and shatter only, not ice sliding. Evaluator should note if ice sliding is absent.
C-14 and C-16 describe specific attack patterns (frost beam, boulder throw) in thresholds but test steps only verify HP values. Evaluator should check for attack pattern properties.
Ice block bridge puzzle from F17 spec has no standalone criterion. C-05 ice block sliding is the underlying mechanic; bridge-building is a level design application of it.
outOfScopeReview: Out of scope list is complete and appropriate. Correctly excludes World 4 (Molten Volcano), Citadel, audio (Sprint 10), VFX polish (Sprint 11), performance (Sprint 12), and World 4-specific mechanics (chain-swinging, rising lava, valves/levers, Magma Slime/Fire Bat/Obsidian Knight).
verdictRationale: All four required revisions from the prior review have been resolved. All three suggestions have been incorporated. The contract now has 31 testable criteria with comprehensive coverage of F17: Frozen Tundra. Test steps use flexible property-path patterns accommodating implementation variation. Regression coverage spans all 6 completed sprints. Minor gaps (attack pattern verification for specific bosses, ice block bridge as standalone criterion) are informational and can be supplemented by evaluator judgment during testing. Contract is accepted for implementation.
